methods.description: Collection of surface sediment samples from various locations in São Vicente to study sediment characteristics and foraminiferal content.

steps.description: Surface sediment samples were taken by opportunity on an excursion to São Vicente during the HOSST Cape Verde Summer School at Mindelo in May and June 2018.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface sediment samples from São Vicente

steps.description: The uppermost 1-2 cm of the surface sediment was scraped off with a spoon, transferred into 100 ml PVC bottles, and immediately preserved and stained with a solution of 2 g rose Bengal in 1 l alcohol (40 %, vodka).
steps.searchable_summary: preserve and stain sediment samples with rose Bengal solution

steps.description: The preservative was exchanged two weeks later to raise the ethanol concentration to >90 % for long-term storage.
steps.searchable_summary: exchange preservative to increase ethanol concentration

steps.description: Global Positioning System (GPS) coordinates and sampling time were retrieved from a mobile phone and noted on the vials.
steps.searchable_summary: record GPS coordinates and sampling time

steps.description: The heights of the samples were obtained from the tide curve for Porto Grande at Mindelo, as calculated by the web application of Xtide for the respective sampling day.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sample heights using tide curve data

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of foraminiferal samples to identify living and dead foraminifera and sort them by species.

steps.description: The foraminiferal samples were prepared and analysed following the procedures described by Schönfeld et al. (2013), and Lübbers & Schönfeld (2018).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and analyze foraminiferal samples

steps.description: The residues 63-2000 μm were split using a HAVER RT 6,5 sample splitter to facilitate microscopic work.
steps.searchable_summary: split sample residues for microscopic analysis

steps.description: One sixteenth was wet picked for rose Bengal stained foraminifera that were alive at the time of sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: wet pick rose Bengal stained foraminifera

steps.description: This split was dried and further subdivided with an Otto microsplitter to an aliquot that contained approximately 100-300 unstained (dead) foraminiferal tests.
steps.searchable_summary: subdivide split foraminifera for unstained tests

steps.description: The specimens from the living fauna and dead assemblages were sorted by species in separate Plummer cell slides, fixed with glue, and counted.
steps.searchable_summary: sort and count foraminifera by species

steps.description: Once rose Bengal stained specimens were not found in the 1/16 split, the remaining 15/16 were treated with a sodium polytungstate solution of 2.303 g cm -3 density to recover all foraminifera from the sample.
steps.searchable_summary: treat remaining sample with sodium polytungstate

steps.description: The floatate and deposited parts of the sample were washed with tap water to remove remaining polytungstate and dried at 50 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and dry sample to remove polytungstate

methods.description: Identification and documentation of foraminiferal species using taxonomic literature and online databases.

steps.description: Species were determined by using both taxonomic literature and online databases.
steps.searchable_summary: identify species using taxonomic literature and databases

steps.description: Preference was given to literature providing high-quality images and accurate taxonomic references for the respective taxon.
steps.searchable_summary: select literature with high-quality images for species identification

steps.description: Images were taken with a Keyence VHX-700 FD digital microscope and a Zeiss LEO 1455VP Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of specimens using digital and SEM microscopes

steps.description: Regional species occurrences were compiled by also considering literature without images but with taxonomic references.
steps.searchable_summary: compile regional species occurrences from literature

methods.method_name: Grainsize Distribution and Coarse Fraction Analysis
methods.description: Analysis of grainsize distribution and coarse fraction composition using sieving and microscopic identification.

steps.description: Grainsize distribution and coarse fraction composition were analysed by using remaining aliquots from the sample residues.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze grainsize distribution using sample residues

steps.description: They were dry sieved with ISO 3310-1 certified sieves with mesh sizes of 125,150,250,355,500, and 1000 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve samples using certified sieves

steps.description: The size fraction >2000 μm was considered as well.
steps.searchable_summary: consider size fraction >2000 μm

steps.description: Coarse fraction analysis was performed by applying the protocol of Sarnthein (1971).
steps.searchable_summary: perform coarse fraction analysis using Sarnthein protocol

steps.description: The subfractions were weighed and further subdivided with an Otto microsplitter.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh and subdivide subfractions using microsplitter

steps.description: Between 103 and 265 grains were identified and counted under a stereo-microscope from each subfraction.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and count grains under stereo-microscope

steps.description: The composition of the sand-sized fraction >63 μm was calculated by combining the proportion of individual components with the weight of the subfractions examined.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate composition of sand-sized fraction >63 μm
